items.summary: In the `createWallet` backend function, ensure the return value includes the full wallet credentials — `address`, `privateKey`, `recoveryPhrase`, and `chain` — exactly once upon successful creation. The private key and recovery phrase must only be present in this single response and must never be persisted in stable storage.
Ensure the `listWallets` backend function returns only wallet addresses (and any non-sensitive metadata such as `chain`). It must never return `privateKey` or `recoveryPhrase` fields.

Update error handling in `createWallet` so that all failure cases return a structured, human-readable error variant instead of propagating raw trap messages. The frontend's `walletErrors.ts` utility should be able to map every returned error variant to a clean user-facing string, eliminating the generic 'Failed to create wallet' message.
